other_observers: Cheri Orwig and Joe Roller; Glenn Walbek found it earlier in the day and seen be several birders
specific_location: Southwest side of Cherry Creek Reservoir just off the old overlook.
description: The belly and breast was white. It had a black medium sized bill. Also had a dark eye. It has a black ear stripe and a black bar going across the back of the neck. The back and top of the head that wasn’t black was very light gray. It has black fairly long wings. The back is about the same colored gray as a Ring-billed Gull. It also had some black feathers on the tertials. It was also slightly smaller than the nearby Ring-billed Gulls.Behaviors: When we saw it the bird was sleeping with a group of gulls which included Bonaparte’s, Ring-billed, and California Gulls.Call: Didn’t hear itPlumage: Juvenile
